steps.title: Identify the subject
steps.content: The subject is “Anthropologist Lívia Barbosa,” which is singular. The prepositional phrase “of the Universidade Federal Fluminense in Brazil” provides additional information but doesn't change the subject's number.

steps.title: Apply subject-verb agreement
steps.content: The rule is subject-verb agreement: a singular subject must take a singular verb form. Choice A 'studies' is the singular present tense form. The other choices fail this requirement: 'are studying' and 'have studied' use plural verb forms, while 'study' is the base form or plural. That matches choice A — A is correct.
